is a type of battery made in large volumes for use by original equipment manufacturers. For example, commodity cells are used in [laptops](/wiki/Laptop "Laptop") and [cell phones](/wiki/Cell_phone "Cell phone"), as the [energy storage](/wiki/Energy_storage "Energy storage") element in its batteries.

A **commodity cell** is a type of battery made in large volumes for use by original equipment manufacturers. For example, commodity cells are used in [laptops](/wiki/Laptop "Laptop") and [cell phones](/wiki/Cell_phone "Cell phone"), as the [energy storage](/wiki/Energy_storage "Energy storage") element in its batteries.

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Commodity_cell&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Production")]

The auto industry battery consortium, [USABC](/wiki/United_States_Council_for_Automotive_Research "United States Council for Automotive Research"), set about to invent automotive batteries made from specialty cells for cars.

[Tesla Motors](/wiki/Tesla_Motors "Tesla Motors") uses commodity cells to make their automotive batteries.

Practically all commodity cells today are made in Asia – mainly Japan, South Korea, and China. There is no significant production anywhere in the US. A modern [lithium ion](/wiki/Lithium_ion "Lithium ion") cell plant – such as those in Japan – is a highly automated affair with very low labor content.
